title: "exec -it" does not work
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I want to exec a interactive shell in a running container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I choose nginx just as an example image, i can reproduce this with any image which keeps running after it was started.

1. `podman run --rm -d --name nginx nginx`

2. `podman exec nginx --rm -it /bin/sh`

**Describe the results you received:**

Exit code: 127

```
Error: executable file not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

An interactive shell.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Issue is persistent.

The following however does work as expected:

```
podman run --rm -it nginx /bin/sh
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.3
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  gitCommit: ""
  goVersion: go1.13.10
  podmanVersion: 1.9.3
host:

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.17, commit: 0f42ac1408cdb791c6a117bb2f231e3a0271c599'
  cpus: 6
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: hostname.domain.local
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:

      host_id: 1000

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.6.11-200.fc31.x86_64
  memFree: 607928320
  memTotal: 33587785728
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.1-1.fc31.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.1
      commit: bbf27c5acd4356edb97fa639b4e15e0cd56a39d5
      libslirp: 4.1.0
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 1
  swapFree: 8580493312
  swapTotal: 8589930496
  uptime: 843h 7m 49.91s (Approximately 35.12 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org

    paused: 0

  graphOptions:

        fusermount3 version: 3.6.2
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.0.0
        FUSE library version 3.6.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.9.3-1.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Standard physical desktop workstation.

comments.body: Looks like the command needs to be `podman exec -it nginx /bin/sh` - as written, you're trying to run the `--rm` command with arguments `-it /bin/sh` inside the container. All arguments to `exec` must be before the container name, everything after is used as the command to execute.
